firmware.name: FluidNC
firmware.description: CNC firmware for ESP32 microcontrollers. Next generation of Grbl_ESP32 with YAML-based configuration, built-in WiFi/Bluetooth, Web UI, and multi-spindle support. G-code is upward-compatible with grbl.

codes.name: Rapid Move
codes.description: Moves to target coordinates at maximum traverse rate. Multiple axis motion is coordinated (straight-line path). Some spindles (lasers) may automatically disable during rapid moves.

examples.description: Rapid move to origin at Z safe height

crossReferences.note: Inherited from grbl. FluidNC supports additional A, B, C rotational axes not available in grbl.
Identical behavior per NIST standard. LinuxCNC may blend rapids with G64 path blending; FluidNC does not.
Smoothieware treats G0 identically with separately configurable per-axis max rates.
In RepRapFirmware, G0 and G1 are identical — both use the F parameter. In FluidNC, G0 always moves at maximum rate.

modeNotes.description: G0 moves automatically disable the laser (S value is preserved but PWM output is 0). This prevents burning during positioning moves.

codes.notes: G0 is modal — once set, subsequent lines without a G command will continue to use G0.

codes.name: Linear Move
codes.description: Controlled straight-line move at the specified feed rate. All axes arrive simultaneously. Error if no feed rate (F) has been set previously.

examples.description: Linear move to X50 Y30 at 500 units/min
Plunge cut to Z-1 at 100 units/min

crossReferences.note: Inherited from grbl. FluidNC adds A, B, C axis support.
Identical behavior. LinuxCNC supports additional U, V, W axes.
RepRapFirmware uses the S parameter on G1 for laser power. FluidNC uses M3/M4 S for laser power, not inline on G1.

modeNotes.description: Laser is active during G1 moves at the power set by M3/M4 S value. With M4 dynamic mode, power scales with speed during acceleration/deceleration.

codes.notes: G1 is modal. The F parameter is also modal — once set, it persists until changed. At startup feed rate is 0; an error occurs if G1 is used without F > 0.

codes.name: Clockwise Arc
codes.description: Clockwise circular or helical interpolation in the currently selected plane (G17/G18/G19). Can use center format (I/J/K offsets) or radius format (R). P parameter specifies number of full turns.

examples.description: CW arc to X10 Y10 with center offset I5 J0
CW arc to X10 Y10 with radius 5

crossReferences.note: Inherited from grbl. FluidNC adds the P parameter for multi-turn arcs.
Identical behavior including P parameter for multi-turn arcs.

codes.notes: Error 33 if the arc geometry cannot fit the specified parameters.

codes.name: Store Predefined Position
codes.description: Stores the current machine position as the G28 predefined position in NVS.

examples.description: Store current position as G28 home position

codes.name: Counter-Clockwise Arc
codes.description: Counter-clockwise circular or helical interpolation in the currently selected plane. Same parameters and behavior as G2 but in the opposite direction.

examples.description: CCW arc to X10 Y10 with center offset I5 J0

crossReferences.note: Inherited from grbl. FluidNC adds the P parameter for multi-turn arcs.

codes.name: Store Predefined Position 2
codes.description: Stores the current machine position as the G30 predefined position in NVS.

examples.description: Store current position as G30 position

codes.name: Dwell
codes.description: Pauses motion for the specified duration. Synchronized command — all buffered G-code completes before the dwell starts.

crossReferences.note: Identical behavior. Both use P in seconds.
LinuxCNC uses P in seconds. Identical behavior.
WARNING: RepRapFirmware uses P in milliseconds and S in seconds. FluidNC uses P in seconds.

codes.name: Set Coordinate System Offset
codes.description: Sets or modifies the offset for a coordinate system. L2 sets the offset value directly; L20 sets it so that the current position becomes the specified value.

parameters.name: L
parameters.description: Mode: L2 = set offset, L20 = set current position

parameters.name: P
parameters.description: Coordinate system: P0 = current, P1-P6 = G54-G59, P7-P9 = G59.1-G59.3

examples.code: G10 L2 P1 X10 Y20 Z0
examples.description: Set G54 offset to X10 Y20 Z0

examples.code: G10 L20 P1 X0 Y0 Z0
examples.description: Set G54 so current position is X0 Y0 Z0

crossReferences.note: LinuxCNC supports additional L1 mode for tool table offsets. FluidNC does not have a tool offset table.

codes.notes: Preferred over G92 for setting work coordinate offsets. Offsets are stored in NVS and persist across reboots.

codes.name: Select XY Plane
codes.description: Selects the XY plane for arc commands (G2/G3). This is the default plane at startup.

codes.name: Select ZX Plane
codes.description: Selects the ZX plane for arc commands (G2/G3).

codes.name: Select YZ Plane
codes.description: Selects the YZ plane for arc commands (G2/G3).

codes.name: Set Units to Inches
codes.description: Sets the unit system to inches for subsequent commands. Only converts X, Y, Z values — A, B, C axes are considered rotary/unitless and are not converted. Configuration values are never converted.
codes.notes: FluidNC's native units are millimeters (G21). G20 only affects G-code interpretation, not config values.

codes.name: Set Units to Millimeters
codes.description: Sets the unit system to millimeters. This is the default at startup and the native unit system of FluidNC.

codes.name: Go to Predefined Position
codes.description: Rapid move to stored predefined position. G28 alone rapids to stored location. G28 with axes rapids to the specified work position first, then rapids to the stored G28 position (only specified axes). G28.1 stores the current machine position as the G28 position.

examples.description: Rapid to Z0, then to stored G28 Z position
Store current position as G28 position

crossReferences.note: Identical behavior — stored position recall, not homing.
CRITICAL: Smoothieware uses G28 for homing, NOT stored position recall. Sending G28 to a Smoothieware machine expecting stored position behavior will home all axes instead.
CRITICAL: RepRapFirmware uses G28 for homing, NOT stored position recall. This is a dangerous incompatibility.

codes.notes: Position is stored in NVS. Only use after homing — stored position is in machine coordinates.

codes.name: Go to Predefined Position 2
codes.description: Same behavior as G28 but uses a separate stored position. G30.1 stores the current machine position as the G30 position.

examples.description: Store current position as G30 position

codes.name: Probe Toward Workpiece (Error on Fail)
codes.description: Probes toward a workpiece, stopping on contact. Errors if probe does not make contact within travel distance. Error if probe is already triggered before command.

examples.description: Probe Z downward at 50mm/min, error if no contact

crossReferences.note: Inherited from grbl. FluidNC adds the P parameter for touch plate offset.

codes.notes: G43 TLO and G92 offsets are accounted for when using the P parameter.

codes.name: Probe Toward Workpiece (No Error on Fail)
codes.description: Probes toward a workpiece, stopping on contact. Does not error if no contact is made — machine simply stops at the target position.

examples.description: Probe Z downward, no error if no contact

codes.name: Probe Away from Workpiece (Error on Fail)
codes.description: Probes away from a workpiece, stopping when contact is lost. Errors if probe is not already in contact before command starts.

examples.description: Probe away from surface, error if probe not initially touching

codes.name: Probe Away from Workpiece (No Error on Fail)
codes.description: Probes away from a workpiece, stopping when contact is lost. Does not error if contact is never lost.

examples.description: Probe away from surface, no error on fail

codes.name: Probe Toward (Incremental/mm, Error on Fail)
codes.description: Like G38.2 but always uses incremental distance mode and millimeters, regardless of the current G20/G21 or G90/G91 state. Does not change the current modal state.

examples.description: Probe 25mm downward in mm/incremental regardless of current mode

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only command (since v3.7.6). No grbl equivalent. Use G38.2 with explicit G91/G21 in grbl.

codes.name: Probe Toward (Incremental/mm, No Error on Fail)
codes.description: Like G38.3 but always uses incremental distance mode and millimeters, regardless of the current G20/G21 or G90/G91 state.

examples.description: Probe 25mm downward, no error on fail

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only command (since v3.7.6). No grbl equivalent.

codes.name: Probe Away (Incremental/mm, Error on Fail)
codes.description: Like G38.4 but always uses incremental distance mode and millimeters, regardless of the current G20/G21 or G90/G91 state.

examples.description: Probe 10mm upward away from surface, error on fail

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only command (since v3.7.6). No grbl equivalent.

codes.name: Probe Away (Incremental/mm, No Error on Fail)
codes.description: Like G38.5 but always uses incremental distance mode and millimeters, regardless of the current G20/G21 or G90/G91 state.

examples.description: Probe 10mm upward, no error on fail

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only command (since v3.7.6). No grbl equivalent.

codes.name: Cutter Compensation Off
codes.description: Turns off cutter radius compensation. This is the default state.

crossReferences.note: Identical — cutter compensation is accepted but not actively implemented in either firmware.
LinuxCNC fully implements cutter compensation (G41/G42). FluidNC only accepts G40.

codes.name: Dynamic Tool Length Offset
codes.description: Creates a tool length offset from the current coordinate system. No motion is generated. Does NOT support the G43 H<tool> format — FluidNC does not maintain a tool offset table.

examples.description: Set tool length offset of -5.2mm in Z

crossReferences.note: Identical behavior — grbl also only supports G43.1, not G43 H.
LinuxCNC supports both G43 H<tool> (from tool table) and G43.1 (dynamic). FluidNC only supports G43.1.

codes.name: Cancel Tool Length Offset
codes.description: Resets the tool length offset to zero.

codes.name: Move in Machine Coordinates
codes.description: Moves to an absolute machine position, ignoring all work coordinate offsets. Not modal — only active for the line it appears on. Does not consider G90/G91.

examples.description: Rapid Z to machine zero (top of travel)

codes.notes: The selected coordinate system is never changed by G53.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 1
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 1. This is the default coordinate system at startup. Offsets are set with G10 and stored in NVS.
codes.notes: Offsets stored in NVS and restored at startup. Read current offsets with $#.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 2
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 2.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 3
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 3.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 4
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 4.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 5
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 5.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 6
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 6.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 7
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 7.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 8
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 8.

codes.name: Select Coordinate System 9
codes.description: Selects work coordinate system 9.

codes.name: Exact Path Mode
codes.description: Selects exact path mode. Accepted for compatibility but does not change FluidNC behavior — the motion planner always operates in its default path mode.

examples.description: Select exact path mode (no-op in FluidNC)

crossReferences.note: grbl also accepts G61 for compatibility but has no configurable path modes.
grblHAL accepts G61. Also supports G61.1 and G64.
LinuxCNC fully implements G61 exact path mode with functional behavior differences from G64 blending mode.

codes.notes: Parsed for G-code compatibility but has no functional effect. FluidNC does not support G61.1 or G64.

codes.name: Cancel Motion Mode
codes.description: Cancels the current motion mode. After G80, any axis words without a motion command (G0, G1, G2, G3) will produce an error. This is a motion mode cancel, not a canned cycle cancel — FluidNC does not support canned cycles.

crossReferences.note: grblHAL uses G80 both as motion mode cancel and canned cycle cancel (G81-G89).
LinuxCNC uses G80 both as motion mode cancel and canned cycle cancel (G73, G76, G81-G89).

codes.notes: FluidNC has no canned cycles, so G80 only cancels the current motion mode (G0/G1/G2/G3).

codes.name: Absolute Distance Mode
codes.description: Axis values in subsequent commands are interpreted as absolute positions.

examples.description: Move to absolute position X10 Y20

codes.name: Incremental Distance Mode
codes.description: Axis values in subsequent commands are interpreted as distances to move from the current position.

codes.name: Incremental Arc Distance Mode
codes.description: Sets arc center offsets (I, J, K) to incremental mode. This is the default and only supported arc distance mode in FluidNC. G90.1 (absolute arc mode) is not supported.

crossReferences.note: Identical behavior — incremental arc mode is the only supported mode.
Identical behavior. grblHAL also does not support G90.1.
LinuxCNC supports both G91.1 and G90.1 (absolute arc mode).

codes.notes: Default mode. G90.1 (absolute arc distance mode) returns an unsupported command error.

codes.name: Coordinate System Offset
codes.description: Sets the current work position to the specified value by applying an offset. Volatile — lost on reboot. Does not affect soft limits.

examples.description: Set current position as work zero

crossReferences.note: WARNING: LinuxCNC persists G92 across reboots. FluidNC does not — G92 offsets are volatile.

codes.notes: The FluidNC wiki recommends G10 instead of G92 for most users, as G10 offsets persist in NVS.

codes.name: Clear Coordinate System Offset
codes.description: Clears all G92 offsets by zeroing them out. Does not affect G54-G59 work coordinate offsets set with G10.

crossReferences.note: Same behavior. LinuxCNC also supports G92.2 (clear without reset) and G92.3 (restore), which FluidNC does not.

codes.name: Inverse Time Feed Rate Mode
codes.description: F word specifies that the move should be completed in 1/F minutes. F must appear on every G1/G2/G3 line when this mode is active.

examples.description: Move to X10, complete in 0.5 minutes

codes.name: Units Per Minute Feed Rate Mode
codes.description: Standard feed rate interpretation — F value specifies speed in units per minute. This is the default mode.

examples.description: Switch to standard feed rate mode

codes.name: Pause (Feed Hold)
codes.description: Puts the machine into hold mode. Resume with the ~ real-time command or a cycle_start_pin signal.

examples.description: Pause program and wait for resume

codes.name: Optional Stop
codes.description: Optional program stop. Parsed for compatibility but does not pause execution in FluidNC. Use M0 for a functional program pause.

crossReferences.note: grbl also parses M1 but it functions identically to M0 (pause).
grblHAL supports M1 with an optional stop control input pin.
LinuxCNC fully supports M1 — pauses only when the optional stop switch is enabled in the UI.

codes.notes: Accepted without error for G-code compatibility but performs no action. Use M0 for program pause.

codes.name: Program End
codes.description: Ends the program and resets modal states to defaults: G54, G17, G90, G94, M5, M9, G40, G1, M48.
codes.notes: Should only be used at the end of a G-code file.

codes.name: Spindle On CW / Laser Constant Power
codes.description: Turns spindle on clockwise at the specified S speed. In laser mode, M3 enables constant power mode — laser output stays at the S value regardless of motion speed.

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Spindle speed (RPM) or laser power level

crossReferences.note: Identical behavior including laser constant power mode.
Smoothieware M3 behavior depends on module configuration rather than $ settings.
RepRapFirmware requires M452 to enable laser mode. Laser power is set inline on G1 S, not on M3.

modeNotes.description: M3 enables constant power mode. Laser stays at set power during moves and dwells. Use M4 for dynamic power that scales with speed.

codes.name: Spindle On CCW / Laser Dynamic Power
codes.description: Turns spindle on counter-clockwise at the specified S speed. In laser mode, M4 enables dynamic power mode — laser output scales proportionally with movement speed during acceleration and deceleration.

parameters.name: S
parameters.description: Spindle speed (RPM) or laser power level

crossReferences.note: Identical behavior including laser dynamic power mode.

modeNotes.description: M4 enables dynamic power mode. Power scales with speed, preventing burns during acceleration/deceleration. Recommended for most laser engraving.

codes.name: Spindle Stop
codes.description: Disables the spindle. Disable behavior (coast-down, braking) is configurable in the spindle config section.

codes.name: Tool Change
codes.description: Changes to the tool specified by T. The T word can appear on the same line or on a previous line. In FluidNC with multiple spindles configured, M6 can switch between spindle types (e.g., laser and router) based on tool number ranges.

crossReferences.note: grbl does not support M6. FluidNC adds tool change support with configurable m6_macro per spindle.
grblHAL supports M6 with tool change modes (manual, semi-automatic, ATC). FluidNC uses m6_macro scripts.
LinuxCNC supports M6 with tool table and tool changer HAL integration. FluidNC uses a simpler macro-based approach.

codes.notes: Each spindle can have its own m6_macro configured in the YAML config file.

codes.name: Set Analog Output (Immediate)
codes.description: Sets a PWM duty cycle on an analog output immediately, without waiting for motion synchronization. Output is configured in the user output section of the YAML config.

examples.description: Immediately set analog output 0 to 75% duty cycle

crossReferences.note: Same behavior — immediate analog output control.

codes.notes: Unlike M67, M68 takes effect immediately rather than being synchronized with motion. Useful for controlling external devices that need instant response.

codes.name: Mist Coolant On
codes.description: Activates the mist coolant digital output. The output can be repurposed for any function (e.g., vacuum, air blast).

crossReferences.note: Identical behavior. grbl requires compile-time option to enable M7.

codes.name: Mist Coolant Off
codes.description: Turns off mist coolant individually without affecting flood coolant. FluidNC-specific command — no standard M-code equivalent exists.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only command. In grbl, use M9 to turn off all coolant (no way to turn off mist individually).
FluidNC-only command. No LinuxCNC equivalent for individual coolant off.

codes.notes: Non-standard — decimal M-codes are a FluidNC extension.

codes.name: Flood Coolant On
codes.description: Activates the flood coolant digital output. The output can be repurposed for any function (e.g., vacuum, air blast).

codes.name: Flood Coolant Off
codes.description: Turns off flood coolant individually without affecting mist coolant. FluidNC-specific command.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only command. In grbl, use M9 to turn off all coolant.

codes.notes: Non-standard — decimal M-codes are a FluidNC extension.

codes.name: All Coolant Off
codes.description: Turns off both mist (M7) and flood (M8) coolant outputs.

codes.name: Program End
codes.description: Ends the program. Same effects as M2 — resets modal states to defaults.

codes.name: Override Parking
codes.description: Enables or disables parking motion override. FluidNC-specific command.

parameters.name: P
parameters.description: P0 = disable parking, P1 = enable parking

codes.name: Set Current Tool
codes.description: Changes the current tool number without performing a tool change macro. Does not affect tool length offset. Used when a tool is installed without FluidNC knowing.

examples.description: Set current tool to 3 without changing

crossReferences.note: Similar behavior — LinuxCNC M61 also sets tool number without motion.

codes.name: Turn On Digital Output (Synchronized)
codes.description: Turns on a digital output pin synchronized with motion. The output changes at the exact point in the motion queue where the command appears.

crossReferences.note: Same behavior — synchronized digital output control per LinuxCNC standard.
No grbl equivalent. grbl does not support digital I/O M-codes.

codes.name: Turn Off Digital Output (Synchronized)
codes.description: Turns off a digital output pin synchronized with motion.

crossReferences.note: Same behavior — synchronized digital output control.

codes.name: Turn On Digital Output (Immediate)
codes.description: Turns on a digital output pin immediately, without waiting for motion synchronization. Timing is unpredictable relative to motion.
codes.notes: The FluidNC wiki recommends avoiding M64/M65 due to unpredictable timing. Use M62/M63 for synchronized output.

codes.name: Turn Off Digital Output (Immediate)
codes.description: Turns off a digital output pin immediately, without waiting for motion synchronization.
codes.notes: Avoid M65 for timing-critical operations. Use M63 instead.

codes.name: Read User Input
codes.description: Reads a digital or analog user input. Partial implementation of LinuxCNC M66 — only immediate mode (L0) is supported. Result is stored in parameter #5399.

examples.description: Read digital input 0 immediately, result in #5399

crossReferences.note: Partial implementation. LinuxCNC supports wait modes (L1-L4). FluidNC only supports L0 (immediate).

codes.name: Set Analog Output (PWM)
codes.description: Sets a PWM duty cycle on an analog output. Output is configured in the user output section of the YAML config.

examples.description: Set analog output 0 to 75% duty cycle

codes.name: Home
codes.description: Runs the homing cycle for all axes, or a single axis if specified. Each axis homes according to its configuration in the YAML config file.

crossReferences.note: Identical behavior. Both support single-axis homing with $H<axis>.
Identical behavior. grblHAL also supports single-axis homing.

codes.name: Clear Alarm / Unlock
codes.description: Clears the alarm state and unlocks the machine. Full command name: $Alarm/Disable.

codes.name: Jog
codes.description: Executes a jog motion. Format: $J=<axes> F<rate>. Jog motions can be cancelled with the 0x85 real-time command. Unlike grbl, FluidNC constrains jogs to the soft limit range instead of throwing an alarm.

crossReferences.note: Similar but FluidNC constrains jogs to soft limits instead of throwing alarm 15.
Similar behavior. grblHAL also supports $J for jogging.

codes.name: Show Grbl Settings
codes.description: Shows grbl-compatible $number settings. Full command name: $GrblSettings/List. In FluidNC, most grbl $number settings are read-only compatibility values — actual configuration is done through YAML config files.

crossReferences.note: In grbl, $$ shows all configurable settings. In FluidNC, most are read-only and actual config is in YAML.
grblHAL uses $$ for live settings. FluidNC uses YAML config instead.

codes.notes: Only $10 (Report/Status) is meaningfully configurable via $number syntax. Use $S for the full FluidNC settings list.

codes.name: Show Coordinate Offsets
codes.description: Shows all coordinate system offsets stored in NVS. Full command name: $GCode/Offsets.

codes.name: Show G-code Modes
codes.description: Shows the current active modal G-code states. Full command name: $GCode/Modes.

examples.description: Show current modal states (e.g., G0 G54 G17 G21 G90 G94 M5 M9 T0 F0 S0)

codes.name: Build Info
codes.description: Shows firmware version, build options, and machine info. Full command name: $Build/Info.

crossReferences.note: Similar — grbl also shows build info with $I.

codes.name: Show All Settings
codes.description: Shows all FluidNC settings and their current values. Full command name: $Settings/List.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC uses $S instead of $$ for the full settings list. $$ in FluidNC shows grbl-compatible settings only.

codes.name: Show Changed Settings
codes.description: Shows only settings that have been changed from their default values. Full command name: $Settings/ListChanged.

codes.name: G-code Check Mode
codes.description: Toggles G-code check mode — a virtual run that parses and validates G-code without producing motion. Full command name: $GCode/Check.

codes.name: Reboot
codes.description: Reboots the ESP32 processor.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only command. grbl does not have a software reboot command.

codes.name: Run File from SD Card
codes.description: Runs a G-code file from the SD card. Full command name: $SD/Run.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only. grbl does not have filesystem support.

codes.name: List SD Card Files
codes.description: Lists all files on the SD card.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only. grbl does not have filesystem support.

codes.name: Delete SD Card File
codes.description: Deletes a file from the SD card.

codes.name: Run File from Local Filesystem
codes.description: Runs a G-code file from the ESP32's local flash filesystem.

codes.name: List Local Filesystem Files
codes.description: Lists all files on the ESP32's local flash filesystem.

codes.name: Delete Local Filesystem File
codes.description: Deletes a file from the ESP32's local flash filesystem.

codes.name: Format Local Filesystem
codes.description: Formats the ESP32's local flash filesystem. Warning: this deletes all files including config.

codes.name: List Alarm Codes
codes.description: Lists descriptions of all alarm codes, or a specific alarm. Full command name: $Alarms/List.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only. grbl does not have a built-in alarm code lookup.

codes.name: List Error Codes
codes.description: Lists descriptions of all error codes, or a specific error. Full command name: $Errors/List.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only. grbl does not have a built-in error code lookup.

codes.name: Disable Motors
codes.description: Disables (unlocks) motors to allow manual movement. Can target a single axis. Full command name: $Motor/Disable.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only. grbl uses $1 (step idle delay) for motor timeout but has no explicit disable command.

codes.name: Enable Motors
codes.description: Enables (locks) motors. Can target a single axis. Full command name: $Motor/Enable.

codes.name: Reinitialize Motors
codes.description: Reinitializes motor drivers. Useful for SPI or UART motor drivers that may need re-initialization. Full command name: $Motors/Init.

codes.name: Run Macro
codes.description: Runs a configured macro file. Macros are defined in the YAML config (macro0-3, after_homing, after_reset, after_unlock). Full command name: $Macros/Run.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only. grbl does not support macros.
grblHAL has a different macro system. FluidNC macros are file-based.

codes.notes: After_homing, after_reset, and after_unlock macros (since v3.7.6) run automatically on those events.

codes.name: Send Alarm
codes.description: Manually triggers an alarm. Useful for testing or conditional G-code macros.

codes.name: Sleep Mode
codes.description: Puts the controller into sleep mode. Full command name: $System/Sleep.

crossReferences.note: Similar — grbl also supports $SLP for sleep mode.

codes.name: Restore Defaults
codes.description: Restores settings to defaults. $RST=$ restores settings, $RST=# restores coordinate offsets, $RST=* restores everything. Full command name: $Settings/Restore.

crossReferences.note: Identical syntax. In FluidNC, this clears NVS but the YAML config file remains.

codes.name: Erase NVS Settings
codes.description: Erases all NVS (Non-Volatile Storage) settings. Full command name: $Settings/Erase.

codes.name: Show Heap Memory
codes.description: Shows free and minimum heap memory on the ESP32. Useful for diagnosing memory issues. Full command name: $Heap/Show.

codes.name: Show NVS Stats
codes.description: Shows NVS usage statistics. Full command name: $Settings/Stats.

codes.name: Show Limit Switch Status
codes.description: Shows real-time status of all limit switches. Full command name: $Limits/Show.

codes.name: GPIO Dump
codes.description: Shows detailed information on all configured GPIO pins. Full command name: $GPIO/Dump.

codes.name: G-code Echo
codes.description: Toggles echoing of received G-code back to the console. Useful for debugging. Set to on or off.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only debugging feature. No grbl equivalent.

codes.name: List WiFi Access Points
codes.description: Scans and lists visible WiFi access points with signal strengths. Shortcut: $ESP410.
codes.notes: FluidNC-specific. Requires ESP32 WiFi to be enabled.

codes.name: List G-code Parameters
codes.description: Lists currently defined G-code parameters (#-variables). Full command name: $Parameters/List.

crossReferences.note: FluidNC-only command. LinuxCNC has parameters but accesses them differently.

codes.notes: FluidNC supports parameters like #<_current_tool>, #<_selected_tool>, #<_metric>, #<_incremental>, #<_abs_x>, #5063 (probe Z), #5399 (M66 result).

codes.name: Status Report Format
codes.description: Configures the status report format. 0 = WPos no buffer, 1 = MPos no buffer, 2 = WPos + buffer, 3 = MPos + buffer. Full command name: $Report/Status.

examples.description: Set status report to MPos without buffer info
Set status report to WPos with buffer info

crossReferences.note: Same setting. The only grbl $number setting that is fully functional in FluidNC.

codes.notes: This is the only grbl $number setting that FluidNC actively uses. All other $ settings are configured via YAML.

codes.name: Show State
codes.description: Shows the current machine state. Full command name: $State.

examples.description: Show current state (Idle, Run, Hold, etc.)

codes.name: Show Startup Messages
codes.description: Re-displays the startup messages that appeared at boot. Full command name: $Startup/Show.

codes.name: Report Interval
codes.description: Controls automatic status reporting interval. Full command name: $Report/Interval.

codes.name: HTTP Command
codes.description: Sends an HTTP request to an external server from within G-code. Advanced feature for integration with external systems.

examples.description: Send HTTP request to external server

codes.notes: FluidNC-only. Requires WiFi connectivity. Shortcut: $HC.

codes.name: UART Passthrough
codes.description: Enables UART passthrough mode for communicating directly with devices connected to a UART port. Full command name: $Uart/Passthrough.

codes.name: XModem Receive
codes.description: Receives a file via the XModem protocol. Full command name: $Xmodem/Receive.

codes.name: XModem Send
codes.description: Sends a file via the XModem protocol. Full command name: $Xmodem/Send.
